{"step_by_step_thinking": "Flumazenil is a benzodiazepine receptor antagonist and is not typically used in the detoxification of chronic alcoholics. Disulfiram is used to maintain abstinence in alcoholics by causing unpleasant reactions if alcohol is consumed. Acamprosate is used to help prevent relapse in alcoholics. Naltrexone is used to reduce cravings and prevent relapse in alcoholics. Therefore, Flumazenil is not used in detoxification of chronic alcoholics.", "answer_choice": "A"}
